steps.title: Use the height formula
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the height is
$$h=\frac{s\sqrt3}{2}$$Substitute the given height into this relationship.

steps.title: Isolate the side length
steps.content:
Solve for $s$ by multiplying by $2$ and then dividing by $\sqrt3$.
$$52\sqrt3=\frac{s\sqrt3}{2}$$
$$104\sqrt3=s\sqrt3$$
$$104=s$$The answer is B.
